NKJV.1: For I want you to know what a great conflict I have for you and those in Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh,
NKJV.2: that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love, and attaining to all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the knowledge of the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ,
NKJV.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
NKJV.4: Now this I say lest anyone should deceive you with persuasive words.
NKJV.5: For though I am absent in the flesh, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.
NKJV.6: As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in Him,
NKJV.7: rooted and built up in Him and established in the faith, as you have been taught, abounding in it with thanksgiving.
NKJV.8: Beware lest anyone cheat you through philosophy and empty deceit, according to the tradition of men, according to the basic principles of the world, and not according to Christ.
NKJV.9: For in Him dwells all the fullness of the Godhead bodily;
NKJV.10: and you are complete in Him, who is the head of all principality and power.
NKJV.11: In Him you were also circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, by putting off the body of the sins of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ,
NKJV.12: buried with Him in baptism, in which you also were raised with Him through faith in the working of God, who raised Him from the dead.
NKJV.13: And you, being dead in your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, He has made alive together with Him, having forgiven you all trespasses,
NKJV.14: having wiped out the handwriting of requirements that was against us, which was contrary to us. And He has taken it out of the way, having nailed it to the cross.
NKJV.15: Having disarmed principalities and powers, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them in it.
NKJV.16: So let no one judge you in food or in drink, or regarding a festival or a new moon or sabbaths,
NKJV.17: which are a shadow of things to come, but the substance is of Christ.
NKJV.18: Let no one cheat you of your reward, taking delight in false humility and worship of angels, intruding into those things which he has not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
NKJV.19: and not holding fast to the Head, from whom all the body, nourished and knit together by joints and ligaments, grows with the increase that is from God.
NKJV.20: Therefore, if you died with Christ from the basic principles of the world, why, as though living in the world, do you subject yourselves to regulations—
NKJV.21: “Do not touch, do not taste, do not handle,”
NKJV.22: which all concern things which perish with the using— according to the commandments and doctrines of men?
NKJV.23: These things indeed have an appearance of wisdom in self-imposed religion, false humility, and neglect of the body, but are of no value against the indulgence of the flesh.

ESV.1: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those at Laodicea and for all who have not seen me face to face,
ESV.2: that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love, to reach all the riches of full assurance of understanding and the knowledge of God's mystery, which is Christ,
ESV.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
ESV.4: I say this in order that no one may delude you with plausible arguments.
ESV.5: For though I am absent in body, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
ESV.6: Therefore, as you received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in him,
ESV.7: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.
ESV.8: See to it that no one takes you captive by philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental spirits  of the world, and not according to Christ.
ESV.9: For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily,
ESV.10: and you have been filled in him, who is the head of all rule and authority.
ESV.11: In him also you were circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, by putting off the body of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ,
ESV.12: having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the powerful working of God, who raised him from the dead.
ESV.13: And you, who were dead in your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, God made alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses,
ESV.14: by canceling the record of debt that stood against us with its legal demands. This he set aside, nailing it to the cross.
ESV.15: He disarmed the rulers and authorities  and put them to open shame, by triumphing over them in him.
ESV.16: Therefore let no one pass judgment on you in questions of food and drink, or with regard to a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath.
ESV.17: These are a shadow of the things to come, but the substance belongs to Christ.
ESV.18: Let no one disqualify you, insisting on asceticism and worship of angels, going on in detail about visions,  puffed up without reason by his sensuous mind,
ESV.19: and not holding fast to the Head, from whom the whole body, nourished and knit together through its joints and ligaments, grows with a growth that is from God.
ESV.20: If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the world, why, as if you were still alive in the world, do you submit to regulations —
ESV.21: “Do not handle, Do not taste, Do not touch”
ESV.22: (referring to things that all perish as they are used) — according to human precepts and teachings?
ESV.23: These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body, but they are of no value in stopping the indulgence of the flesh.

NASB.1: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have on your behalf and for those who are at Laodicea, and for all those who have not personally seen my face,
NASB.2: that their hearts may be encouraged, having been knit together in love, and [attaining] to all the wealth that comes from the full assurance of understanding, [resulting] in a true knowledge of God's mystery, [that is], Christ [Himself],
NASB.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
NASB.4: I say this so that no one will delude you with persuasive argument.
NASB.5: For even though I am absent in body, nevertheless I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good discipline and the stability of your faith in Christ.
NASB.6: Therefore as you have received Christ Jesus the Lord, [so] walk in Him,
NASB.7: having been firmly rooted [and now] being built up in Him and established in your faith, just as you were instructed, [and] overflowing with gratitude.
NASB.8: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deception, according to the tradition of men, according to the elementary principles of the world, rather than according to Christ.
NASB.9: For in Him all the fullness of Deity dwells in bodily form,
NASB.10: and in Him you have been made complete, and He is the head over all rule and authority;
NASB.11: and in Him you were also circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, in the removal of the body of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ;
NASB.12: having been buried with Him in baptism, in which you were also raised up with Him through faith in the working of God, who raised Him from the dead.
NASB.13: When you were dead in your transgressions and the uncircumcision of your flesh, He made you alive together with Him, having forgiven us all our transgressions,
NASB.14: having canceled out the certificate of debt consisting of decrees against us, which was hostile to us; and He has taken it out of the way, having nailed it to the cross.
NASB.15: When He had disarmed the rulers and authorities, He made a public display of them, having triumphed over them through Him.
NASB.16: Therefore no one is to act as your judge in regard to food or drink or in respect to a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath day--
NASB.17: things which are a [mere] shadow of what is to come; but the substance belongs to Christ.
NASB.18: Let no one keep defrauding you of your prize by delighting in self-abasement and the worship of the angels, taking his stand on [visions] he has seen, inflated without cause by his fleshly mind,
NASB.19: and not holding fast to the head, from whom the entire body, being supplied and held together by the joints and ligaments, grows with a growth which is from God.
NASB.20: If you have died with Christ to the elementary principles of the world, why, as if you were living in the world, do you submit yourself to decrees, such as,
NASB.21: 'Do not handle, do not taste, do not touch!'
NASB.22: (which all [refer] [to] things destined to perish with use)-- in accordance with the commandments and teachings of men?
NASB.23: These are matters which have, to be sure, the appearance of wisdom in self-made religion and self-abasement and severe treatment of the body, [but are] of no value against fleshly indulgence.

NLT.1: I want you to know how much I have agonized for you and for the church at Laodicea, and for many other believers who have never met me personally.
NLT.2: I want them to be encouraged and knit together by strong ties of love. I want them to have complete confidence that they understand God’s mysterious plan, which is Christ himself.
NLT.3: In him lie hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
NLT.4: I am telling you this so no one will deceive you with well-crafted arguments.
NLT.5: For though I am far away from you, my heart is with you. And I rejoice that you are living as you should and that your faith in Christ is strong.
NLT.6: And now, just as you accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord, you must continue to follow him.
NLT.7: Let your roots grow down into him, and let your lives be built on him. Then your faith will grow strong in the truth you were taught, and you will overflow with thankfulness.
NLT.8: Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ.
NLT.9: For in Christ lives all the fullness of God in a human body.
NLT.10: So you also are complete through your union with Christ, who is the head over every ruler and authority.
NLT.11: When you came to Christ, you were “circumcised,” but not by a physical procedure. Christ performed a spiritual circumcision — the cutting away of your sinful nature.
NLT.12: For you were buried with Christ when you were baptized. And with him you were raised to new life because you trusted the mighty power of God, who raised Christ from the dead.
NLT.13: You were dead because of your sins and because your sinful nature was not yet cut away. Then God made you alive with Christ, for he forgave all our sins.
NLT.14: He canceled the record of the charges against us and took it away by nailing it to the cross.
NLT.15: In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross.
NLT.16: So don’t let anyone condemn you for what you eat or drink, or for not celebrating certain holy days or new moon ceremonies or Sabbaths.
NLT.17: For these rules are only shadows of the reality yet to come. And Christ himself is that reality.
NLT.18: Don’t let anyone condemn you by insisting on pious self-denial or the worship of angels, saying they have had visions about these things. Their sinful minds have made them proud,
NLT.19: and they are not connected to Christ, the head of the body. For he holds the whole body together with its joints and ligaments, and it grows as God nourishes it.
NLT.20: You have died with Christ, and he has set you free from the spiritual powers of this world. So why do you keep on following the rules of the world, such as,
NLT.22: Such rules are mere human teachings about things that deteriorate as we use them.
NLT.23: These rules may seem wise because they require strong devotion, pious self-denial, and severe bodily discipline. But they provide no help in conquering a person’s evil desires.

WEB.1: For I desire to have you know how greatly I struggle for you, and for those at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;
WEB.2: that their hearts may be comforted, they being knit together in love, and gaining all riches of the full assurance of understanding, that they may know the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ,
WEB.3: in whom are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden.
WEB.4: Now this I say that no one may delude you with persuasiveness of speech.
WEB.5: For though I am absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, rejoicing and seeing your order, and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.
WEB.6: As therefore you received Christ Jesus, the Lord, walk in him,
WEB.7: rooted and built up in him, and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving.
WEB.8: Be careful that you don’t let anyone rob you through his philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the elements of the world, and not after Christ.
WEB.9: For in him all the fullness of the Godhead dwells bodily,
WEB.10: and in him you are made full, who is the head of all principality and power;
WEB.11: in whom you were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands, in the putting off of the body of the sins of the flesh, in the circumcision of Christ;
WEB.12: having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.
WEB.13: You were dead through your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh. He made you alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses,
WEB.14: wiping out the handwriting in ordinances which was against us; and he has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross;
WEB.15: having stripped the principalities and the powers, he made a show of them openly, triumphing over them in it.
WEB.16: Let no one therefore judge you in eating, or in drinking, or with respect to a feast day or a new moon or a Sabbath day,
WEB.17: which are a shadow of the things to come; but the body is Christ’s.
WEB.18: Let no one rob you of your prize by a voluntary humility and worshiping of the angels, dwelling in the things which he has not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
WEB.19: and not holding firmly to the Head, from whom all the body, being supplied and knit together through the joints and ligaments, grows with God’s growth.
WEB.20: If you died with Christ from the elements of the world, why, as though living in the world, do you subject yourselves to ordinances,
WEB.22: (all of which perish with use), according to the precepts and doctrines of men?
WEB.23: Which things indeed appear like wisdom in self-imposed worship, and humility, and severity to the body; but aren’t of any value against the indulgence of the flesh.

CSB17.1: For I want you to know how greatly I am struggling <sup>ⓔ</sup> for you, for those in Laodicea, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and for all who have not seen me in person.
CSB17.2: I want their hearts to be encouraged <sup>ⓖ</sup> and joined together in love, so that they may have all the riches of complete understanding and have the knowledge of God’s mystery <sup>ⓗ</sup>  — Christ. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: In him are hidden all the treasures of wisdom <sup>ⓙ</sup> and knowledge. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.4: I am saying this so that no one will deceive you with arguments that sound reasonable.
CSB17.5: For I may be absent in body, but I am with you in spirit, <sup>ⓛ</sup> rejoicing to see how well ordered you are and the strength of your faith in Christ.
CSB17.6: So then, just as you have received <sup>ⓜ</sup> Christ Jesus as Lord, <sup>ⓝ</sup> continue to live in him,
CSB17.7: being rooted and built up in him <sup>ⓐ</sup> and established in the faith, just as you were taught, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and overflowing with gratitude.
CSB17.8: Be careful that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deceit based on human tradition, <sup>ⓒ</sup> based on the elements of the world, rather than Christ. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.9: For the entire fullness <sup>ⓔ</sup> of God’s nature dwells bodily <sup>[2]</sup> in Christ,
CSB17.10: and you have been filled by him, who is the head <sup>ⓕ</sup> over every ruler and authority.
CSB17.11: You were also circumcised in him with a circumcision not done with hands, by putting off the body of flesh, <sup>ⓖ</sup> in the circumcision of Christ, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.12: when you were buried with him <sup>ⓘ</sup> in baptism, in which you were also raised with him <sup>ⓙ</sup> through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.13: And when you were dead in trespasses and in the uncircumcision of your flesh, he made you alive with him and forgave us all our trespasses.
CSB17.14: He erased the certificate of debt, with its obligations, that was against us and opposed to us, and has taken it away by nailing it to the cross. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.15: He disarmed the rulers and authorities and disgraced them publicly; he triumphed over them in him. <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.16: Therefore, don’t let anyone judge <sup>ⓝ</sup> you in regard to food and drink <sup>ⓐ</sup> or in the matter of a festival or a new moon <sup>ⓑ</sup> or a Sabbath day. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.17: These are a shadow <sup>ⓒ</sup> of what was to come; <sup>ⓓ</sup> the substance is <sup>[5]</sup> Christ. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.18: Let no one condemn <sup>[6]</sup> you <sup>ⓕ</sup> by delighting in ascetic practices <sup>ⓖ</sup> and the worship of angels, claiming access to a visionary realm. Such people are inflated <sup>ⓗ</sup> by empty notions of their unspiritual <sup>[7]</sup> mind.
CSB17.19: He doesn’t hold on to the head, from whom the whole body, <sup>ⓘ</sup> nourished and held together by its ligaments and tendons, grows with growth from God.
CSB17.20: If you died with Christ <sup>ⓙ</sup> to the elements of this world, why do you live as if you still belonged to the world? Why do you submit to regulations:
CSB17.22: All these regulations refer to what is destined to perish by being used up; they are human commands and doctrines.
CSB17.23: Although these have a reputation for wisdom <sup>ⓚ</sup> by promoting self-made religion, false humility, and severe treatment of the body, they are not of any value in curbing self-indulgence. <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>

AMP.1: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those [believers] at Laodicea, and for all who [like yourselves] have never seen me face to face.
AMP.2: [For my hope is] that their hearts may be encouraged as they are knit together in [unselfish] love, so that they may have all the riches that come from the full assurance of understanding [the joy of salvation], resulting in a true [and more intimate] knowledge of the mystery of God, that is, Christ,
AMP.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge [regarding the word and purposes of God].
AMP.4: I say this so that no one will deceive you with persuasive [but thoroughly deceptive] arguments.
AMP.5: For even though I am absent [from you] in body, nevertheless I am with you in spirit, delighted to see your good discipline [as you stand shoulder to shoulder and form a solid front] and to see the stability of your faith in Christ [your steadfast reliance on Him and your unwavering confidence in His power, wisdom, and goodness].
AMP.6: Therefore as you have received Christ Jesus the Lord, walk in [union with] Him [reflecting His character in the things you do and say—living lives that lead others away from sin],
AMP.7: having been deeply rooted [in Him] and now being continually built up in Him and [becoming increasingly more] established in your faith, just as you were taught, and overflowing in it with gratitude.
AMP.8: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deception [pseudo-intellectual babble], according to the tradition [and musings] of mere men, following the elementary principles of this world, rather than following [the truth—the teachings of] Christ.
AMP.9: For in Him all the fullness of Deity (the Godhead) dwells in bodily form [completely expressing the divine essence of God].
AMP.10: And in Him you have been made complete [achieving spiritual stature through Christ], and He is the head over all rule and authority [of every angelic and earthly power].
AMP.11: In Him you were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands, but by the [spiritual] circumcision of Christ in the stripping off of the body of the flesh [the sinful carnal nature],
AMP.12: having been buried with Him in baptism and raised with Him [to a new life] through [your] faith in the working of God, [as displayed] when He raised Christ from the dead.
AMP.13: When you were dead in your sins and in the uncircumcision of your flesh (worldliness, manner of life), God made you alive together with Christ, having [freely] forgiven us all our sins,
AMP.14: having canceled out the certificate of debt consisting of legal demands [which were in force] against us and which were hostile to us. And this certificate He has set aside and completely removed by nailing it to the cross.
AMP.15: When He had disarmed the rulers and authorities [those supernatural forces of evil operating against us], He made a public example of them [exhibiting them as captives in His triumphal procession], having triumphed over them through the cross.
AMP.16: Therefore let no one judge you in regard to food and drink or in regard to [the observance of] a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath day.
AMP.17: Such things are only a shadow of what is to come and they have only symbolic value; but the substance [the reality of what is foreshadowed] belongs to Christ.
AMP.18: Let no one defraud you of your prize [your freedom in Christ and your salvation] by insisting on mock humility and the worship of angels, going into detail about visions [he claims] he has seen [to justify his authority], puffed up [in conceit] by his unspiritual mind,
AMP.19: and not holding fast to the head [of the body, Jesus Christ], from whom the entire body, supplied and knit together by its joints and ligaments, grows with the growth [that can come only] from God.
AMP.20: If you have died with Christ to the elementary principles of the world, why, as if you were still living in the world, do you submit to rules and regulations, such as,
AMP.21: "Do not handle [this], do not taste [that], do not [even] touch!"?
AMP.22: (these things all perish with use)—in accordance with the commandments and teachings of men.
AMP.23: These practices indeed have the appearance [that popularly passes as that] of wisdom in self-made religion and mock humility and severe treatment of the body (asceticism), but are of no value against sinful indulgence [because they do not honor God].

MSG.1: I want you to realize that I continue to work as hard as I know how for you, and also for the Christians over at Laodicea. Not many of you have met me face-to-face, but that doesn't make any difference. Know that I'm on your side, right alongside you. You're not in this alone.
MSG.2: I want you woven into a tapestry of love, in touch with everything there is to know of God. Then you will have minds confident and at rest, focused on Christ, God's great mystery.
MSG.3: All the richest treasures of wisdom and knowledge are embedded in that mystery and nowhere else. And we've been shown the mystery!
MSG.4: I'm telling you this because I don't want anyone leading you off on some wild-goose chase, after other so-called mysteries, or "the Secret."
MSG.5: I'm a long way off, true, and you may never lay eyes on me, but believe me, I'm on your side, right beside you. I am delighted to hear of the careful and orderly ways you conduct your affairs, and impressed with the solid substance of your faith in Christ.
MSG.6: My counsel for you is simple and straightforward: Just go ahead with what you've been given. You received Christ Jesus, the Master; now live him.
MSG.7: You're deeply rooted in him. You're well constructed upon him. You know your way around the faith. Now do what you've been taught. School's out; quit studying the subject and start living it! And let your living spill over into thanksgiving.
MSG.8: Watch out for people who try to dazzle you with big words and intellectual double-talk. They want to drag you off into endless arguments that never amount to anything. They spread their ideas through the empty traditions of human beings and the empty superstitions of spirit beings. But that's not the way of Christ.
MSG.9: Everything of God gets expressed in him, so you can see and hear him clearly. You don't need a telescope, a microscope, or a horoscope to realize the fullness of Christ, and the emptiness of the universe without him.
MSG.10: When you come to him, that fullness comes together for you, too. His power extends over everything.
MSG.11: Entering into this fullness is not something you figure out or achieve. It's not a matter of being circumcised or keeping a long list of laws. No, you're already in—insiders—not through some secretive initiation rite but rather through what Christ has already gone through for you, destroying the power of sin.
MSG.12: If it's an initiation ritual you're after, you've already been through it by submitting to baptism. Going under the water was a burial of your old life; coming up out of it was a resurrection, God raising you from the dead as he did Christ.
MSG.13: When you were stuck in your old sin-dead life, you were incapable of responding to God. God brought you alive—right along with Christ! Think of it! All sins forgiven,
MSG.14: the slate wiped clean, that old arrest warrant canceled and nailed to Christ's Cross.
MSG.15: He stripped all the spiritual tyrants in the universe of their sham authority at the Cross and marched them naked through the streets.
MSG.16: So don't put up with anyone pressuring you in details of diet, worship services, or holy days.
MSG.17: All those things are mere shadows cast before what was to come; the substance is Christ.
MSG.18: Don't tolerate people who try to run your life, ordering you to bow and scrape, insisting that you join their obsession with angels and that you seek out visions. They're a lot of hot air, that's all they are.
MSG.19: They're completely out of touch with the source of life, Christ, who puts us together in one piece, whose very breath and blood flow through us. He is the Head and we are the body. We can grow up healthy in God only as he nourishes us.
MSG.20: So, then, if with Christ you've put all that pretentious and infantile religion behind you, why do you let yourselves be bullied by it?
MSG.21: "Don't touch this! Don't taste that! Don't go near this!"
MSG.22: Do you think things that are here today and gone tomorrow are worth that kind of attention?
MSG.23: Such things sound impressive if said in a deep enough voice. They even give the illusion of being pious and humble and ascetic. But they're just another way of showing off, making yourselves look important.

NIV.1: I want you to know how much I am struggling for you and for those at Laodicea, and for all who have not met me personally.
NIV.2: My purpose is that they may be encouraged in heart and united in love, so that they may have the full riches of complete understanding, in order that they may know the mystery of God, namely, Christ,
NIV.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
NIV.4: I tell you this so that no one may deceive you by fine-sounding arguments.
NIV.5: For though I am absent from you in body, I am present with you in spirit and delight to see how orderly you are and how firm your faith in Christ is.
NIV.6:
Freedom From Human Regulations Through Life With Christ
So then, just as you received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to live in him,
NIV.7: rooted and built up in him, strengthened in the faith as you were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
NIV.8: See to it that no one takes you captive through hollow and deceptive philosophy, which depends on human tradition and the basic principles of this world rather than on Christ.
NIV.9: For in Christ all the fullness of the Deity lives in bodily form,
NIV.10: and you have been given fullness in Christ, who is the head over every power and authority.
NIV.11: In him you were also circumcised, in the putting off of the sinful nature, not with a circumcision done by the hands of men but with the circumcision done by Christ,
NIV.12: having been buried with him in baptism and raised with him through your faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
NIV.13: When you were dead in your sins and in the uncircumcision of your sinful nature, God made you alive with Christ. He forgave us all our sins,
NIV.14: having canceled the written code, with its regulations, that was against us and that stood opposed to us; he took it away, nailing it to the cross.
NIV.15: And having disarmed the powers and authorities, he made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross.
NIV.16: Therefore do not let anyone judge you by what you eat or drink, or with regard to a religious festival, a New Moon celebration or a Sabbath day.
NIV.17: These are a shadow of the things that were to come; the reality, however, is found in Christ.
NIV.18: Do not let anyone who delights in false humility and the worship of angels disqualify you for the prize. Such a person goes into great detail about what he has seen, and his unspiritual mind puffs him up with idle notions.
NIV.19: He has lost connection with the Head, from whom the whole body, supported and held together by its ligaments and sinews, grows as God causes it to grow.
NIV.20: Since you died with Christ to the basic principles of this world, why, as though you still belonged to it, do you submit to its rules:
NIV.21: “Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!”?
NIV.22: These are all destined to perish with use, because they are based on human commands and teachings.
NIV.23: Such regulations indeed have an appearance of wisdom, with their self-imposed worship, their false humility and their harsh treatment of the body, but they lack any value in restraining sensual indulgence.

NRSVCE.1: For I want you to know how much I am struggling for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for all who have not seen me face to face.
NRSVCE.2: I want their hearts to be encouraged and united in love, so that they may have all the riches of assured understanding and have the knowledge of God’s mystery, that is, Christ himself,
NRSVCE.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
NRSVCE.4: I am saying this so that no one may deceive you with plausible arguments.
NRSVCE.5: For though I am absent in body, yet I am with you in spirit, and I rejoice to see your morale and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
NRSVCE.6: As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord, continue to live your lives in him,
NRSVCE.7: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.
NRSVCE.8: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental spirits of the universe, and not according to Christ.
NRSVCE.9: For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily,
NRSVCE.10: and you have come to fullness in him, who is the head of every ruler and authority.
NRSVCE.11: In him also you were circumcised with a spiritual circumcision, by putting off the body of the flesh in the circumcision of Christ;
NRSVCE.12: when you were buried with him in baptism, you were also raised with him through faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
NRSVCE.13: And when you were dead in trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, God made you alive together with him, when he forgave us all our trespasses,
NRSVCE.14: erasing the record that stood against us with its legal demands. He set this aside, nailing it to the cross.
NRSVCE.15: He disarmed the rulers and authorities and made a public example of them, triumphing over them in it.
NRSVCE.16: Therefore do not let anyone condemn you in matters of food and drink or of observing festivals, new moons, or sabbaths.
NRSVCE.17: These are only a shadow of what is to come, but the substance belongs to Christ.
NRSVCE.18: Do not let anyone disqualify you, insisting on self-abasement and worship of angels, dwelling on visions, puffed up without cause by a human way of thinking,
NRSVCE.19: and not holding fast to the head, from whom the whole body, nourished and held together by its ligaments and sinews, grows with a growth that is from God.
NRSVCE.20: If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the universe, why do you live as if you still belonged to the world? Why do you submit to regulations,
NRSVCE.21: “Do not handle, Do not taste, Do not touch”?
NRSVCE.22: All these regulations refer to things that perish with use; they are simply human commands and teachings.
NRSVCE.23: These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-imposed piety, humility, and severe treatment of the body, but they are of no value in checking self-indulgence.

NET.1: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for those who have not met me face to face.
NET.2: My goal is that their hearts, having been knit together in love, may be encouraged, and that they may have all the riches that assurance brings in their understanding of the knowledge of the mystery of God, namely, Christ,
NET.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
NET.4: I say this so that no one will deceive you through arguments that sound reasonable.
NET.5: For though I am absent from you in body, I am present with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your morale and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
NET.6: Therefore, just as you received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to live your lives in him,
NET.7: rooted and built up in him and firm in your faith just as you were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
NET.8: Be careful not to allow anyone to captivate you through an empty, deceitful philosophy that is according to human traditions and the elemental spirits of the world, and not according to Christ.
NET.9: For in him all the fullness of deity lives in bodily form,
NET.10: and you have been filled in him, who is the head over every ruler and authority.
NET.11: In him you also were circumcised — not, however, with a circumcision performed by human hands, but by the removal of the fleshly body, that is, through the circumcision done by Christ.
NET.12: Having been buried with him in baptism, you also have been raised with him through your faith in the power of God who raised him from the dead.
NET.13: And even though you were dead in your transgressions and in the uncircumcision of your flesh, he nevertheless made you alive with him, having forgiven all your transgressions.
NET.14: He has destroyed what was against us, a certificate of indebtedness expressed in decrees opposed to us. He has taken it away by nailing it to the cross.
NET.15: Disarming the rulers and authorities, he has made a public disgrace of them, triumphing over them by the cross.
NET.16: Therefore do not let anyone judge you with respect to food or drink, or in the matter of a feast, new moon, or Sabbath days —
NET.17: these are only the shadow of the things to come, but the reality is Christ!
NET.18: Let no one who delights in humility and the worship of angels pass judgment on you. That person goes on at great lengths about what he has supposedly seen, but he is puffed up with empty notions by his fleshly mind.
NET.19: He has not held fast to the head from whom the whole body, supported and knit together through its ligaments and sinews, grows with a growth that is from God.
NET.20: If you have died with Christ to the elemental spirits of the world, why do you submit to them as though you lived in the world?
NET.21: “Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!”
NET.22: These are all destined to perish with use, founded as they are on human commands and teachings.
NET.23: Even though they have the appearance of wisdom with their self-imposed worship and false humility achieved by an unsparing treatment of the body — a wisdom with no true value — they in reality result in fleshly indulgence.
